alerts.title: Iran launches missile barrage at Israel; ceasefire collapses amid Beirut strikes
alerts.summary: Iran reportedly fired multiple missiles at Israel in the first direct attack since ceasefire began in April, reportedly in response to Israeli strikes on Beirut that killed at least 2 civilians, per NPR, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, and AP News. Simultaneous Israeli bombardment of Hezbollah-controlled southern Beirut suburbs signals rapid escalation across Israel-Lebanon and Iran-Israel fronts with ceasefire framework failing.

alerts.title: Iranian air defense downs US/Israeli drone over Iraq amid missile attack
alerts.summary: Iranian air defenses shot down an Hermes-900 reconnaissance drone that reportedly crashed in Iraqi territory during Iran's missile barrage on Israel, per Middle East Spectator Telegram (~31-37K views). Interception suggests Iran operating advanced air defense systems and monitoring US-Israeli surveillance posture in real-time during active escalation.

alerts.title: Trump urges Netanyahu restraint after Iran missile attack; diplomacy signals emerge
alerts.summary: US President Trump publicly called Netanyahu 'right now' to tell him not to retaliate for Iran's missile strikes and stated 'That's enough, back to the table and make a deal,' per Times of Israel and Axios. Trump also expressed unhappiness with Israeli strikes on Lebanon, signaling potential US pivot toward de-escalation and negotiation amid active hostilities.

alerts.title: Three US carrier strike groups maintain forward posture amid escalating theater tensions
alerts.summary: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower, USS Abraham Lincoln, and USS Gerald R. Ford carrier groups remain deployed across Red Sea, Arabian Sea, and Mediterranean respectively, per live OSINT asset tracking. USS Bataan amphibious ship and destroyers USS Carney, Mason, and Laboon concentrated in Red Sea maintain US readiness for sustained multi-front operations if ceasefire fully collapses.

alerts.title: US presses IAEA to demand Iran accounting for bombed nuclear sites and enriched uranium
alerts.summary: US has drafted IAEA resolution forcing Iran to publicly disclose status of nuclear facilities and enriched uranium stockpile following escalation, per Reuters, Jerusalem Post, and Al-Monitor. Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment with 1-2 week estimated weapons-grade breakout window while international verification remains blocked by conflict, creating acute proliferation risk.

analysis_note: US-Iran direct conflict has breached ceasefire framework with Iranian missile barrage on Israel and Israeli retaliation in Beirut; concurrent nuclear verification gaps, Trump de-escalation signals, and forward-deployed US maritime assets create volatile multi-vector crisis requiring urgent diplomatic intervention.
